content:
How Hackers Exploit the OSI Model?
*****For defensive learning only*****
Layer 7 Application Hackers exploit weak passwords SQL Injection Cross Site Scripting XSS File Upload Vulnerabilities Remote Code Execution
Layer 6 Presentation Hackers target weak encryption Exploit insecure SSL TLS configuration Steal unencrypted sensitive data
Layer 5 Session Hackers steal session cookies Session Hijacking Session Fixation Replay Attacks
Layer 4 Transport Hackers perform TCP SYN Flood attacks UDP Flood attacks Port Scanning Connection Reset attacks
Layer 3 Network Hackers perform IP Spoofing Packet Sniffing on insecure networks ICMP attacks Routing attacks
Layer 2 Data Link Hackers use ARP Spoofing MAC Flooding MAC Address Spoofing VLAN Hopping
Layer 1 Physical Hackers steal devices Connect rogue hardware Cut network cables Install hardware keyloggers Tamper with networking equipment
Summary
Layer 7 Web application attacks
Layer 6 Encryption attacks
Layer 5 Session attacks
Layer 4 Port scanning and DoS attacks
Layer 3 IP and routing attacks
Layer 2 ARP and MAC attacks
Layer 1 Physical access attacks
